calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Continued To Oct-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ava Whitmire in Propria Persona. Danielle Vogl for Mary Nelson. Vanessa Nelson & Brigitte Scott also present.

The Court stated that this is Ms. Whitmire's petition, that it continues to not be in proper shape to proceed, that there is an objection stating that there is no need to probate, that we now have a creditor's claim, that Ms. Nelson has not filed a competing petition and asked why nothing has been filed to either clear up the issues or proceed. Ms. Whitmire stated that she has spoken with an attorney, that there are 5 wills and a trust, that the assets are in the trust, that she has asked counsel for the original trust but has not gotten it and that counsel says they do not have the original trust. The Court stated that if the assets are in the trust they are not in the estate. Ms. Vogl stated that the creditor's claim is secured by the deed of trust and that all assets are held in the trust. The Court stated that it does not have a petition that can be acted on. Ms. Whitmire stated that they received a letter that there is a family trust, that there should be a Bishop Nelson Trust and a Nelson Family Trust but that counsel says they do not have the original. The Court stated that if there is a trust and the assets are in the trust you may not need to probate and directed Ms. Whitmire to talk to her attorney regarding how to proceed. Ms. Whitmire asked about elder abuse and was directed again to speak to her attorney. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to October 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the Court stated that it expects that either an amended petition will be filed or taken off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-03-2016 Continued To Sep-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ava Whitmire in Propria Persona. Dannielle Vogl for (objector) Mary Nelson.

The Court stated that this is a petition for letters of administration, that it presents a petition to administer the estate in intestacy but there is a will as stated by the objector. Ms. Whitmire stated that she asked her stepmother for the will and was told that a will could not be found, that she was aware of wills existing, that her father had dementia and then cancer and that in December of 2015 another will making changes was executed. The Court stated that there is a will, that it needs to be the basis of the petition. Ms. Whitmire responded that there were deeds filed and asked how her father could sign them if he had dementia. The Court stated that there are presumptions that the deeds that exist have validity, that the problem is that we cannot go forward with a petition that does not refer to the will, that first Ms. Whitmire needs to obtain a lawyer, that her petition as presented says that it is an intestate estate and that if she wants to pursue she will need to amend the petition. Ms. Whitmire stated that there are 3 different wills and that she is petitioning the will signed when her father had dementia. The Court stated that if the dispute is on the outcome regarding anything in the estate or if the argument is ultimately that probate is needed for the deeds to be recognized it probably would not get litigated on that petition, that the only petition before the court needs to be amended and that it may well make sense to talk to each other regarding if it makes sense to litigate in a forum other than Probate. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to September 14, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the Court stating that if this is going forward petitioner will need to get an amendment filed and served quickly.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
